calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Paltenghi from Bowles & Verna.
John Sappington (Administrator) also present.

Mr. Paltenghi stated that this started out like ordinary services, that there was a tremendous amount of work done to get everything in place, that it was very complex, that it was a lot of work at the start and that it continued on. Mr. Paltenghi also stated that they represented the Special Administrator, that the Decedent had a business that provided programs during the holidays, that Mr. Farber and Mr. Sappington handled the business side, and that he handled the probate. In addition, Mr. Paltenghi stated that Ms. Harrington did a lot of work for the probate, that they had to identify accounts, that there were big events, and that they dealt with business leases, employee issues and personal issues. Furthermore, Mr. Paltenghi also stated that the name of the business was Sebastian Presents, that they set up large parties for New Year's Eve and Christmas, that they worked with large bands and that there are no objections that he is aware of. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-15-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-01-2018 Continued To Aug-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for John Sappington.

Ms. Harrington stated that the petition was uploaded this morning, that her file got corrupted and that she had to start all over. Ms. Harrington also stated that she spoke with the Guardianship Attorney, that the Attorney was reticent to filing of a temporary guardianship, that she explained to him that the guardianship is necessary, that her Client said he sent all of the documents to the Attorney and that she is waiting for a hearing date. Status on close of estate is continued to August 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Harrington that she can not close this estate out without having a guardianship in place and that it is concerned about the funds.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Continued To Aug-01-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared by telephone for John Sappington.

The Court raised the issue of large amounts of money that have gone out of the Estate without Court order which include funds that went out to a Minor without a guardianship. Ms. Harrington stated that she now has the accounting, that she can have the petition on file in two weeks, that the Administrator has Counsel to establish a guardianship in Sonoma and that she has been in contact with the Administrator to not release any of those funds. Ms. Harrington also stated that the Administrator is fully bonded, that in her review nothing has been stolen, that she would like an order to transfer the funds into a guardianship and that the funds are in an UTMA account. The Court stated that it will not approve a distribution to someone who is not a Guardian. Ms. Harrington stated that she will talk with the Guardianship Attorney about establishing a temporary guardianship. Status on close of estate is continued to August 1, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (201 Harrison St, Unit 913)
Off Calendar Oct-02-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for John Sappington.

The sale was confirmed at $825,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed August 28, 2017. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
